title: Skills CLI Test Execution Flow
description: End-to-end integration test lifecycle: create temp environment, invoke CLI, assert output and filesystem state, then clean up.
key_participants: fs helpers (mkdtempSync, mkdirSync, writeFileSync, readdirSync, readFileSync, rmSync)
helper_signatures: run(args: string, opts?: object): string  — execSync('node CLI args', {encoding:'utf8', timeout:60000, ...opts})
createFakeSkills(root, skills=['audit','polish','impeccable'], providers=['.claude']): void  — creates <root>/<provider>/skills/<skill>/SKILL.md for each combination

test_suites.lifecycle: beforeAll: mkdtempSync + createFakeSkills(tmp, 3 skills, ['.claude','.cursor']); afterAll: rmSync

tests.name: renames folders with prefix
steps: writeFileSync(_test_rename.mjs) — inline helper script with prefixSkillContent()
readdirSync(.claude/skills) → expect i-audit, i-polish, i-impeccable present; audit, polish absent

tests.name: prefixed SKILL.md has correct name and cross-references

test_suites.lifecycle: beforeAll: mkdtempSync + git init + write stale SKILL.md for audit & impeccable; afterAll: rmSync

tests.name: downloads universal bundle and updates skills
update added new skills that were not present before
steps: expect(skills.length).toBeGreaterThan(5)  // bundle has ~20 skills, only 2 existed

test_suites.lifecycle: beforeAll: mkdtempSync + write prefixed skills (i-audit, i-polish, i-impeccable) in .claude; afterAll: rmSync

tests.name: detectPrefix finds prefix from skill names
undo removes prefix from folders and content
steps: writeFileSync(_undo.mjs) — iterates prefixed entries, renameSync to unprefixed, rewrite SKILL.md (name + /refs + 'the X skill')
execSync('node _undo.mjs') → JSON array of skill names
expect audit, polish, impeccable present; i-audit absent
readFileSync(audit/SKILL.md) → expect name:audit, /audit, /polish, 'the impeccable skill'; no /i-audit

tests.name: re-applying prefix restores original state
steps: writeFileSync(_reprefix.mjs) — same logic as rename test
expect i-audit, i-polish, i-impeccable present; audit absent

test_suites.suite: skills install: full e2e with npx skills (conditional)
test_suites.lifecycle: SKIPPED if npx skills --version fails; beforeAll: mkdtempSync + git init; afterAll: rmSync
test_suites.condition: hasNpxSkills = execSync('npx skills --version') succeeds → describeNpx = describe or describe.skip

tests.name: installs skills into a fresh project
install with --prefix= renames all skills
steps: iterate ['.claude','.cursor','.gemini','.codex','.agents','.kiro'] to find non-empty skills dir
expect all skills start with 'x-' (prefixed.length === skills.length)

activity_flow_summary: Check if npx skills available → set hasNpxSkills flag (try/catch execSync)
[beforeAll] git init and/or createFakeSkills or write stale skills
Optionally write inline helper .mjs script to tmp dir
Invoke CLI via run() (execSync node CLI <args>) OR execSync helper script
Assert stdout content (toContain) and/or filesystem state (readdirSync, readFileSync)
IF hasNpxSkills is false → npx suite is SKIPPED entirely
key_branching_conditions: hasNpxSkills: true → run npx e2e suite; false → skip.describe
Skill dir already contains impeccable or *-impeccable → CLI outputs 'already installed', exits early
lstatSync(src).isSymbolicLink() → symlink path vs rename path in undo logic
filesystem_structure_created_by_createFa: SKILL.md body: cross-references to other skills via /skillname and 'the X skill'
important_notes: All tests use real temp directories (mkdtempSync). No mocking of fs or child_process. Inline helper .mjs scripts are written to tmp and cleaned up by afterAll rmSync. Timeout: most tests 15000ms, update 60000ms, npx e2e 90000ms. Test runner: bun:test.
